main.deledge_before: Before uninstalling Microsoft Edge, make sure you have another browser. Do you want to go to the Google Chrome page before uninstalling?

main.deledge_tooltip: Complete removal of Microsoft Edge, including update services and scheduler tasks. Edge WebView2 is not affected, so critical system components remain functional. Effectively, only the browser itself is removed - however, perform actions at your own risk.

main.deledge_done: Microsoft Edge uninstallation has been completely finished.
main.index_tooltip: Recommended for older computers and laptops with HDD - can significantly reduce disk load and increase system responsiveness. It may speed up file explorer on any device, but searching in the system and explorer will become slower.

main.label: Performance
main.info:
With MakuTweaker, you can increase your laptop's battery life by software-limiting the processor's power level.
On desktop PCs, this helps reduce heat, power consumption, and fan noise, making the system quieter and more stable.
The lower the percentage you set, the lower the performance will be, but the longer the battery life.
Please note that after unplugging the laptop from the charger,
you may need to set the limit again.
main.flyout: Processor performance limit has been set to

main.label: Process Management

main.info: MakuTweaker allows you to block the launch of specified processes and applications. List them separated by commas, and the program will automatically apply restrictions. To unblock a process, remove it from the list and save the changes.

main.label: Settings
main.cfg_info: You can save selected MakuTweaker settings to a .mktw file to later import and apply them on a new computer or share with friends. A restart may be required after applying.
main.cfg_svsuccess: Settings were successfully saved to the file.
main.cfg_ldsuccess: Settings from the file were successfully applied. A restart may be needed.

main.cfg_error1: An error occurred while reading the file.
main.credL:
Version:
Idea and Development:
Program Icon:
Special Thanks:

main.sfc:
One of the two standard Windows recovery options.
Quick scan and repair of system files without an internet connection.
main.dism:
One of the two standard Windows recovery options.
DISM is deeper and more reliable, as it connects to Windows Update to download fixed files from Microsoft servers.
main.sticky:
Pressing the Shift key multiple times opens the
"Sticky Keys" window, which can be annoying during gaming.
MakuTweaker allows you to disable this feature.
main.coreisol: Core Isolation reduces PC performance (especially on low-end systems) and may even break virtual machines. It is recommended to disable Core Isolation together with Virtualization Based Security if you experience issues launching VMware Workstation virtual machines or severe micro-stuttering in games and across the system. Regular users do not need these features, and even on modern PCs this may increase performance by up to 5%. Core Isolation and VBS add an extra security layer, but Windows Defender already provides sufficient protection.
main.vbs:
It is recommended to disable Core Isolation together with Virtualization Based Security if you experience issues launching VMware Workstation virtual machines or severe micro-stuttering in games and across the system. Regular users do not need these features, and even on modern PCs this may increase performance by up to 5%.
Core Isolation and VBS add an extra security layer, but Windows Defender already provides sufficient protection.
main.duac:
When launching an application that requires administrator privileges,
for example, MakuTweaker - a window appears asking "Do you want to allow this app to make changes to your device?". Many users find this annoying, so MakuTweaker allows disabling this prompt. Keep in mind: with UAC disabled, all applications run with administrator privileges. Some Python scripts require normal privileges, as they may malfunction when run as administrator. On Windows 11, after disabling UAC, drag-and-drop to the taskbar will stop working. This is a Windows 11 limitation and is not related to MakuTweaker.
main.smartscr: When launching unsigned applications, such as MakuTweaker, a warning window may appear with alarming text like "Windows protected your PC" or even "Malicious file. This app may harm your device", which is often not true. This window commonly appears when launching repacks or cracked software, attempting to prevent piracy. The SmartScreen component also affects download warnings in Microsoft Edge. After disabling it, downloads will start immediately without additional confirmation dialogs.
main.hybern: Disabling hibernation can free up several dozen gigabytes on the system drive, depending on your installed RAM. Hibernation is similar to sleep mode. While sleep stores data in RAM, hibernation saves everything to the system drive. On SSDs, this may increase wear. For this reason, Microsoft hid this option by default, yet the hibernation file still occupies disk space.
main.swap:
Disabling the page file is strongly not recommended.
The page file is a system mechanism that uses disk space as additional virtual memory when RAM is insufficient. It helps prevent crashes and program closures under heavy load. Disabling the page file may only be justified on devices with small SSDs (up to 120 GB), as this can free up to 20 GB of disk space.
main.oldloader: In older Windows bootloaders (before Windows 7), you could repeatedly press F8 during startup to enter Safe Mode. In modern Windows versions, you must interrupt the boot process three times manually, which significantly slows down access to Safe Mode when troubleshooting startup issues. You can enable the old bootloader design and ensure quick access to Safe Mode.
main.additional:
To enter advanced startup options, enabling this feature is not required.
You can open the Start menu and, while holding Shift, click "Restart".
main.chkdsk: Whenever an HDD develops a bad sector, Windows marks the partition as "dirty". The system checks such drives at every startup, and unfortunately, modern Windows versions allow only one second to skip the scan, which is not enough for normal system access. Scanning a slow 5400 RPM drive may take up to an entire day. Extending the timeout from 1 second to 1 minute will save time and allow you to deal with the problematic disk.
main.bitlocker:
Starting with Windows 11 23H2, any edition automatically encrypts all drives with BitLocker after signing into a Microsoft account.
This may be risky, as losing access to your Microsoft account could result in permanent data loss, even without the ability to reinstall the system.
main.advanced: During system startup and shutdown, you will see detailed information about service initialization. On Insider builds, this can be useful to identify which service slows down system boot.
main.photow: The classic Windows Photo Viewer from Windows Vista was removed in Windows 10. Even on Windows 11, it opens instantly. However, it may sometimes display a phantom low-memory error even when sufficient RAM is available.
main.powershell: Sets the PowerShell execution policy to Unrestricted. Allows running any scripts, including those downloaded from the internet. If script execution is disabled, scripts (for example, Node.js-based ones) may show the error "cannot be loaded because running scripts is disabled on this system."
main.xbox: Windows 10 and 11 LTSC editions lack Xbox components, which may cause the error "You'll need a new app to open this ms-gamingoverlay link" when launching games. After disabling the popup, the error will no longer appear.
main.hyperv: Forcibly disabling the Hyper-V component may help remove performance limitations in VMware Workstation virtual machines.
main.directplay: DirectPlay is required to run older games such as GTA Vice City.
main.bing: In modern versions of Windows 10 and Windows 11, when you type in the Start menu search, the system may show results from Bing. If this feature is disabled, information about the found file will be displayed on the right.
main.MakuTweakerProcessMGR:
The "Process Management" tab is the best alternative to the Windows Task Manager.

The implementation is very simple and user-friendly: system processes that cannot be terminated anyway are hidden. Only the processes that the user actually started themselves are displayed.

All processes are automatically sorted from highest to lowest RAM usage. You can also set a minimum threshold for displaying processes - from 50 MB to 2 GB. This is convenient for filtering the list and leaving only the "heaviest" processes - for example video editing software, virtual machines, or games.

The main feature of process management in MakuTweaker is the checkbox at the bottom called "Show only frozen processes". If a window in Windows is marked as not responding and turns white, you can enable this option to display only such windows, quickly find the frozen process, and terminate it.

You can also right-click a process to open the context menu. Unlike the Task Manager, it allows you to immediately open the process "File location". And when you click "End process", the entire process tree is terminated instantly.

The RAM usage calculation system in MakuTweaker differs from the Task Manager -
in MakuTweaker the values are more realistic.
This is because the program accounts for system resources being used as well as extra memory reserved by applications that the Task Manager hides. Thanks to this, for example, you can see the actual memory usage of video editing programs that reserve RAM for preview rendering. This applies to any applications.
main.MakuTweakerProcessMGR1: Click the button to expand the help window.
main.ttl:
Some mobile carriers, particularly in Russia (e.g., MTS, Beeline, MegaFon, Tele2, Yota) often block or charge an additional fee for sharing unlimited internet from a smartphone to a PC. They track this using the TTL parameter,
which defaults to 128 on Windows and 64 on phones.
Fixing the TTL at 65 means that when passing through the phone, the value will decrease by 1, and the carrier will receive a standard "smartphone" packet with a value of 64. They will see that the network is supposedly being shared with another smartphone rather than a computer, so this can save you from unexpected charges.

MakuTweaker makes absolutely no guarantee that this will work.
It was requested by MakuTweaker users from Russia.
